business_ideas.idea: Polymarket: crypto prediction market for elections, sports, science events
Jingles revival: bring back 1990s-style brand earworms for modern products
McDonald's/Subway brand song integration: short 5-second hook at ad end

predictions.text: Polymarket will grow massive as prediction markets replace traditional polling for accuracy

disagreements: Sam uncomfortable promoting gambling products; Shaan argues wisdom-of-crowds accuracy and information value justify it despite addiction risks
dynamics: Sam leads with jingle revival thesis; Shaan adds prediction market analysis. Discussion pivots between media/advertising to gambling ethics. Playful energy with underlying tension around moral responsibility for platform usage.
frameworks: Jingle effectiveness: requires cringiness + repetition + brand mentions + call to action
Phonetic loop: brains naturally memorize rhythmic/rhyming content better than prose
Earworm mechanism: small repetitive snippet gets stuck in memory via neuroscience
Automated Market Maker (AMM): odds determined by volume, not central odds maker
Wisdom of crowds: betting markets more predictive than polls because money on line
Prediction market as media: data embeddable in articles makes predictions newsworthy
Corporate social responsibility vs profitability: tension between impact and business model
Effective altruism: make capital to give away vs immediate impact work
learnings: Jingles declined from 15% of commercials (1990s) to 1% today, but brains unchanged, not fashion trend away
Earworms and music stick better than speech; can recall capital songs from childhood decades later
Repetition (frequency) critical for jingle stickiness; needs 300+ daily plays to work
Prediction markets historically more accurate than polls because money incentivizes honesty
Crypto prediction markets enable global betting on information discovery and forecasting
Account managers gamification and personal attention drive addiction similar to mobile games
Whale hunters in mobile gaming model replicate in sports betting: 95% users spend nothing, 5% spend everything
Leverage and regulatory defensibility matter more in financial services than for consumer products

banger_quotes.quote: I want my baby back, baby back, baby back: Ribs commercial jingle
$5, $5, footlong: Subway jingle call to action
Jingles work. The brain hasn't changed. This is fashion, not utility.
Wisdom of crowds beats individual expertise when money is on the line

numbers_dropped: Polymarket 2024: 400M betting volume on track for 1B annually
Polymarket monthly volume: ~50M (10M this week in some reports)
2024 election bet: 237M volume, Trump 61%, Biden 19%, Kamala 10%
Top bettor (Revenge Tour 1984): 1.69M in positions, up 1M profit, 169M traded
Polymarket funding: $75M Series C from Founders Fund, Joe Gebbia, Vitalik Buterin
DraftKings woman case: $150k earner, $350k debt, 50+ addiction signals from account manager
notable: Tension between celebration of Polymarket innovation and concerns about gambling addiction and account manager manipulation tactics; Sam expresses moral discomfort with product design to exploit users.
